name: Fortune Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix egg whites, sugar, and salt thoroughly with a spoon.
2. Add the remaining ingredients (except fortunes) to the mixture. Cover with plastic wrap and chill for 30 minutes.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a baking sheet. Have clean white cotton gloves ready for folding and shaping cookies, or use two pieces of folded paper towel as an alternative to protect fingers from hot cookies. Also have some clean muffin tins ready to hold baked cookies while they cool.
4. Shape and bake 2 cookies at a time. For each cookie, drop 1 teaspoon batter onto the baking sheet and spread with the back of a spoon to form a 3-inch circle.
5. Bake for 3-5 minutes at 350°F, or until edges turn light brown.
6. Remove one cookie from the baking sheet using a wide spatula to the counter top. Place a fortune paper across the center of the cookie. Using gloves or paper towel, fold the edge of the cookie over to make a semicircle. Hold the cookie on the ends and place the middle of the folded edge over the muffin pan; bend the ends down. Carefully place the folded cookie into a muffin cup to cool.
7. Repeat the process until all cookies are cooled.
8. Makes 36 cookies.
Serving: 2 cookies - 80 calories, 1/2 starch/bread, 1 fat exchange; 2 grams protein, 6 grams fat, 6 grams carbohydrate; 84 mg sodium; 0 cholesterol
